title: Amazon Q4 2025 Earnings Release
content: SEATTLE — Amazon.com, Inc. today announced financial results for its fourth quarter ended December 31, 2025. Net sales increased 10% to $187.4 billion in Q4 2025, compared with $169.9 billion in Q4 2024. Operating income was $21.2 billion in Q4 2025, compared with $13.2 billion in Q4 2024. Net income was $20.0 billion, or $1.86 per diluted share, in Q4 2025, compared with $10.6 billion in Q4 2024. Andy Jassy, President and CEO, said: 'We had a great Q4 to cap off an exceptional 2025 for Amazon. AWS continues to accelerate with AI as a meaningful tailwind — AWS grew 19% and is now a $115 billion annualized revenue run rate business.' North America net sales were $115.6 billion (+10%), including $56.1 billion from AWS, Advertising, and Subscription services combined. International net sales were $43.6 billion (+8% excluding FX). AWS revenue was $28.8 billion (+19%), driven by generative AI workloads from enterprise and startup customers. AWS operating income was $10.7 billion, representing a 37.1% operating margin, up from 29.6% a year ago. Advertising Services grew 18% to $17.3 billion. Subscription Services (Prime, apps, digital content) grew 10% to $11.7 billion. Full-year 2025 net sales were $638 billion, up 11%. Amazon employed approximately 1.55 million full- and part-time workers globally at year-end 2025.
